header_info.title: Global Geothermal Power Tracker - March 2026
header_info.citation: Recommended Citation: "Global Energy Monitor, Global Geothermal Tracker, March 2026 release"  (See the CC license for attribution requirements if sharing or adapting the data set.)
header_info.contact: Contact: Sophia Bauer, Global Geothermal Power Tracker Project Manager, Global Energy Monitor - sophia.bauer@globalenergymonitor.org

entries.name: operating
entries.definition: Commercial operation date achieved.

entries.name: construction
entries.definition: Equipment installation has begun (not just clearing/roads).

entries.name: pre-construction
entries.definition: Projects that are actively moving forward in seeking governmental approvals, land rights, or financing.

entries.name: announced
entries.definition: Projects that been publicly reported but have not yet moved actively forward by applying for permits or seeking land, material, or financing. Examples: (1) projects are the potential “Unit 2” at a location where “Unit 1” is currently under development, (2) Projects that are described in long-range company or governmental planning documents.

entries.name: shelved
entries.definition: Suspension of the project operation has been announced.

entries.name: shelved - inferred 2 y
entries.definition: The project is inferred to be shelved because, or no progress has been observed for at least two years.

entries.name: mothballed
entries.definition: Disused but not dismantled.

entries.name: retired
entries.definition: Dismantled.

entries.name: cancelled
entries.definition: A cancellation announcement has been made.

entries.name: cancelled - inferred 4 y
entries.definition: The project is inferred to be cancelled because, or no progress has been observed for at least four years

entries.name: dry steam
entries.definition: Projects that use steam directly from a geothermal reservoir to turn a turbine.

entries.name: flash steam - single
entries.definition: Projects where the high-pressure geothermal fluid is converted to steam only once.

entries.name: flash steam - double
entries.definition: Projects where the remaining water after the first flash is still warm enough to flash again and used to run a second, low-pressure steam turbine.

entries.name: flash steam - triple
entries.definition: Such systems use three flashes to generate three different steam pressures: high, intermediate, and low. The steam flows are then used to rotate two or more turbines.

entries.name: flash steam - unknown
entries.definition: Projects where the high-pressure geothermal fluid is converted to steam but the number of flashes is unknown.

entries.name: binary cycle
entries.definition: Projects that transfer the heat from geothermal fluid to another liquid. The heat causes the second liquid to turn to steam, which is used to drive a turbine.

entries.name: EGS (Enhanced Geothermal Systems)
entries.definition: Projects that creates machine-made geothermal reservoirs in regions where there is naturally hot rock but insufficient permeability of fluid for a traditional geothermal power plant.

entries.name: AGS (Advanced Geothermal Systems)
entries.definition: Projects that use deep, large, artificial closed-loop circuits in which a working fluid is circulated and heated by sub-surface rocks through conductive heat transfer.

entries.name: GGS (Geopressured Geothermal Systems)
entries.definition: Pojects that repurpose fracking technology to extract thermal energy from miles below the Earth’s surface.

entries.name: unknown type
entries.definition: The type of geothermal power is not specified.

entries.name: Date Last Researched
entries.definition: Date that this project was last researched by the researcher.

entries.name: Country/Area
entries.definition: Country/area the project is located in (Unabbreviated).

entries.name: Project Name
entries.definition: Commonly used name.
entries.notes: Preference is for a government provided name. Without that information, the name the owner or operating company uses was taken.

entries.name: Unit Name
entries.definition: Common name of the project unit.
entries.notes: Roman numerals are changed to numbers. If the Owner uses non-sequential numbers or other characters, those are used.

entries.name: Project Name in Local Language / Script
entries.definition: Commonly used name in the locally used script.
entries.notes: Left blank for local languages that use the roman/latin alphabet. Otherwise local name in local script provided if possible.

entries.name: Other Name(s)
entries.definition: Alternative names, including the name in local script.

entries.name: Unit Capacity (MW)
entries.definition: Nameplate capacity, or best available data, of the unit in megawatts rounded to whole number.

entries.name: Type
entries.definition: See rows 25-32 above.

entries.name: Status
entries.definition: See rows 16-23 above.

entries.name: Start year
entries.definition: Year the project is or is expected to be commissioned.
entries.notes: Units: yyyy. Latest date was taken when original data source provided a range. Start years not included for projects that are cancelled or shelved.

entries.name: Retired year
entries.definition: Year the plant is or is expected to be taken offline.
entries.notes: Units: yyyy. This includes mothballed plants - whenever the unit stops operating.

entries.name: Operator
entries.definition: The company that operates the project.
entries.notes: The company that performs day-to-day operations and maintenance of the project.

entries.name: Operator Name in Local Language / Script
entries.definition: Company name that operates the project in the locally used script.
entries.notes: Left blank for local languages use the roman/latin alphabet. Otherwise operator name in local script included if possible.

entries.name: Owner
entries.definition: The company that directly owns the plant. When owned by a special purpose vehicle, the next level of ownership is provided.

entries.name: Owner Name in Local Language / Script
entries.definition: Company name that directly owns the project in the locally used script.
entries.notes: Left blank for local languages that use the roman/latin alphabet. Otherwise direct owner name included in local script if possible.

entries.name: Green Hydrogen Producing
entries.definition: Is green hydrogen produced by the project.

entries.name: Data Center PPA
entries.definition: Is the unit connected to a data center through a PPA

entries.name: Employment Notes
entries.definition: Qualitative notes on employment numbers related to construction or operation of the project.

entries.name: Latitude
entries.definition: Latitude, assumed WGS84 (google maps), of the project unit.

entries.name: Longitude
entries.definition: Longitude, assumed WGS84 (google maps), of the project unit.

entries.name: Location accuracy
entries.definition: Location is approximate based on using nearest city or other information in data sources, or location is exact when explicitly provided in data source.

entries.name: City
entries.definition: City, town, village, or township (China) where the project is located.

entries.name: Local Area
entries.definition: County, taluk, or district (China) where the project is located.

entries.name: Major Area
entries.definition: Prefecture, district, or municipality (China) where the project is located.

entries.name: State/Province
entries.definition: Subnational unit where the project is located.

entries.name: Subregion
entries.definition: Subregion location of the project based on United Nations definition.

entries.name: Region
entries.definition: Region location of the project based on United Nations definition.

entries.name: Other IDs (location)
entries.definition: Location identifier from external datasets.

entries.name: Other IDs (unit/phase)
entries.definition: Unit identifier from external datasets.

entries.name: GEM Location ID
entries.definition: GEM generated ID unique for each project.

entries.name: GEM Unit ID
entries.definition: GEM generated ID unique for each unit.

entries.name: Wiki URL
entries.definition: URL of existing GEM.wiki page.
